mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 2% signal aggressive monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of corporate earnings. This produces positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as market reprices equity risk premium downward.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls below 2.0% and remains there for at least 1 month
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of Feb 2009 indicates recent sharp cuts from prior levels
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle following financial shock
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 2.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window post-trigger activation.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rising above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline across consumer-sensitive sectors.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.8% as of Feb 2009 but trending upward from crisis onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of months when UNRATE > 4.5%, or correlation between UNRATE change and forward SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month by more than 2% signals demand destruction that cascades through earnings revisions for consumer discretionary and industrial firms, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month percentage change falls below -2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 697557 million as of Feb 2009 during financial crisis trough
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or Spearman rank correlation between RSAFS momentum and forward SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines for two consecutive months signals supply-side contraction and capacity utilization collapse. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as profit margins compress and capital expenditure plans are cut.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 103.49 as of Feb 2009, down sharply from pre-crisis levels
regime_hint: supply-side recession with falling capacity utilization
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances following 2-month consecutive INDPRO declines, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) and unemployment rate move in opposite directions with PAYEMS declining while UNRATE rises. This joint deterioration signals accelerating labor market weakness that triggers equity risk-off positioning, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month by more than 100k while UNRATE rises by more than 0.2pp in same month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 156419k and UNRATE at 3.8% as of Feb 2009, both deteriorating
regime_hint: synchronized labor market shock during financial crisis
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term (PAYEMS decline × UNRATE rise) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) both decline simultaneously, signaling synchronized demand destruction across residential and consumer sectors. This dual contraction produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as forward earnings estimates are revised downward across construction, materials, and discretionary sectors.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines month-over-month by more than 5% AND RSAFS declines month-over-month by more than 1.5% in same month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1452k and RSAFS at 697557M as of Feb 2009, both in crisis contraction
regime_hint: synchronized demand shock across housing and consumer spending
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of co-triggered instances, or the joint interaction signal shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) declines month-over-month while federal funds rate remains above 3%, signaling deflation risk with monetary policy still restrictive. This regime produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as markets price in policy error and recession deepening.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month percentage change is negative AND FEDFUNDS > 3.0%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 306.27 and FEDFUNDS at 5.33% as of Feb 2009
regime_hint: deflation risk with lagged monetary tightening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 70% of co-triggered instances, or the interaction term shows Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) growth rate falls below 0% (recession) while housing starts (HOUST) remain depressed below 1500k units. This regime of negative growth with housing collapse produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations deteriorate and capital allocation shifts to defensive positioning.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate is negative AND HOUST < 1500k
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 20386.5B and HOUST at 1452k as of Feb 2009 during recession trough
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances when both conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window during recession periods.
